skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X with posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI. Apps: x/post-tweet, x/post-create (with media), x/post-like, x/post-retweet, x/dm-send, x/user-follow. Capabilities: post tweets, schedule content, like posts, retweet, send DMs, follow users, get profiles. Use for: social media automation, content scheduling, engagement bots, audience growth, X API. Triggers: twitter api, x api, tweet automation, post to twitter, twitter bot, social media automation, x automation, tweet scheduler, twitter integration, post tweet, twitter post, x post, send tweet
skill.summary: Automate Twitter/X posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: infsh-skills

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 45 potential issues. After semantic evaluation: 36 external_command detections are false positives (bash examples in markdown documentation). 2 high-severity cryptographic algorithm detections are false positives (pattern matching misidentified YAML description and markdown table). 7 network detections are legitimate (inference.sh API URLs). No malicious intent detected. This is legitimate documentation for a Twitter/X automation CLI tool.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Misidentified Cryptographic Pattern
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:27 as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. After evaluation, these are false positives. Line 3 is YAML frontmatter description containing plain text. Line 27 is markdown table syntax. No cryptographic code or algorithms present.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual inspection confirms no cryptographic algorithms exist. Pattern matcher likely misidentified 'API' keyword or table delimiters as cryptographic patterns.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Bash Command Documentation
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 36 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are bash command examples in markdown code blocks (belt app run commands). No actual backtick execution or command injection vulnerabilities exist. Commands are hardcoded strings with JSON inputs, not user-controlled.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matching on 'belt' CLI syntax triggered false positives. Documentation examples use hardcoded arguments, no user input injection vector exists.

content.user_title: Automate Twitter/X posts, likes, and follows
content.value_statement: Managing Twitter/X manually takes hours each day. This skill automates posting, engagement, and user management through the inference.sh CLI, saving time while maintaining authentic presence.
actual_capabilities: Post text tweets and media-attached posts to Twitter/X
Like and retweet existing posts by ID
Retrieve tweet details and user profiles
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI (belt) installation and authentication
Limited to Twitter/X platform operations via inference.sh API
Media posts require publicly accessible image/video URLs

use_cases.title: Schedule social media content
use_cases.description: Social media managers use this skill to automate posting content calendars. Claude generates text content, then this skill posts it at optimal times.

use_cases.title: Engage with followers automatically
use_cases.description: Community managers automatically like and retweet mentions, follow new followers, and send welcome DMs using scheduled scripts.

use_cases.title: Create AI-generated content pipelines
use_cases.description: Combine with image/video generation skills to automatically post AI-created content. Generate image, extract URL, post to Twitter all in one workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Post a simple tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to post a tweet that says: {tweet_text}

prompt_templates.title: Post with AI-generated image
prompt_templates.prompt: First generate an image with AI, then use twitter-automation to post it with the caption: {caption}

prompt_templates.title: Engage with mentions
prompt_templates.prompt: Find recent mentions of our account and use twitter-automation to like each one and follow the authors.

prompt_templates.title: Content calendar automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a content calendar for the next 7 days. For each post, generate appropriate text and use twitter-automation to queue them with different timestamps.

output_examples.input: Post a tweet announcing our new feature launch
output_examples.output:
Successfully posted tweet: 'We're excited to announce our new feature launch! Check it out at example.com/feature 🚀'
Tweet ID: 1234567890

output_examples.input: Follow the users who recently mentioned our account
output_examples.output:
Following 5 users: @user1, @user2, @user3, @user4, @user5
Sent welcome DMs to all new follows

output_examples.input: Post an AI-generated sunset image with caption
output_examples.output:
Generated image URL: https://cdn.example.com/image.jpg
Posted tweet with media: 'Golden hour over the mountains 🌅'
Tweet ID: 0987654321

best_practices: Authenticate with inference.sh CLI before using skill commands
Use publicly accessible URLs for media attachments
Space out automated actions to avoid rate limiting
anti_patterns: Do not post identical content multiple times per hour
Do not mass-follow users in rapid succession
Do not send unsolicited direct messages to users

faq.question: How do I set up the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: Run 'belt login' after installing the CLI. See installation guide at raw.githubusercontent.com/inference-sh/skills/refs/heads/main/cli-install.md

faq.question: Can I post images with tweets?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the x/post-create app with a text field and media_url pointing to a publicly accessible image or video.

faq.question: How do I get a tweet ID to like or retweet?
faq.answer: Use x/post-get with the tweet URL or ID to retrieve details, or get user mentions through x/user-get.

faq.question: Is this skill free to use?
faq.answer: The skill is free, but requires inference.sh account and may have API usage limits based on your plan.

faq.question: How do I send a direct message?
faq.answer: Use x/dm-send with recipient_id (user ID, not username) and text content. Recipients must allow DMs from everyone.

faq.question: Can I combine this with AI image generation?
faq.answer: Yes. Generate images with other inference.sh apps, then pass the resulting URL to x/post-create for posting.
